finding.summary: Bass guitar track volume is set too high (+5 dB), resulting in near-clipping true peak (-0.3 dB) and insufficient headroom for mixing.
finding.probable_cause: Track fader boosted to +5 dB, likely to achieve perceived loudness in isolation without accounting for downstream mix bus headroom.
finding.confidence_reason: Measured true peak (-0.3 dB) and sample peak (-0.7 dB) are close to 0 dBFS, directly attributable to the +5 dB track fader. Silence fraction is low (0.05), so peaks are not diluted by silence.

evidence_refs.description: Track fader set to +5 dB, reinforcing all levels.
True peak at -0.3 dB, critically close to digital clipping.
Sample peak at -0.7 dB, also very high.

proposal.reason: Reduce track volume to provide at least 3 dB headroom before mixing, preventing intersample clipping and allowing room for processing.
